title: Houses for Rent in Fairfax, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Fairfax, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Explore houses for rent in Fairfax, Cleveland (ZIP 44104), near the Cleveland Clinic campus. Rent ranges, RTA access, and Section 8-friendly homes.
answerBox: Fairfax is an east-side Cleveland neighborhood in ZIP code 44104, adjacent to the Cleveland Clinic's main campus. It's one of the areas where we work with the most rental homes, with 2- and 3-bedroom houses typically running $700-$1,300 a month, and every home accepts Housing Choice Vouchers.

body: Fairfax is an east-side Cleveland neighborhood in **ZIP code 44104**, directly adjacent to the **Cleveland Clinic's main campus**, one of the region's largest employers. The neighborhood's housing stock is predominantly early-1900s single-family and two-family homes on grid-pattern residential streets running east from the Clinic campus toward Kinsman.

The neighborhood has also been shaped by the Opportunity Corridor boulevard project, which runs along Fairfax's southern edge connecting University Circle to I-490 near East 105th Street, and by ongoing Cleveland Clinic campus expansion along Carnegie and Euclid Avenues. For renters working in health care or education, the short commute to the Clinic or nearby University Circle institutions is one of the neighborhood's practical draws.

Fairfax and the surrounding Central/Kinsman ZIP 44104 area is one of the neighborhoods where we work with the most rental homes citywide — [see current availability](/p/book-showing) or [apply online](/apply) if you're ready to move forward. Every home we work with in this area is Section 8-friendly and HUD-inspection-ready.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent a house in Fairfax?
body: Across our Cleveland selection, rents range from about **$700 to $1,800 a month**, with a typical rent near **$1,000**, and 44104 is one of our higher-volume ZIP codes. Citywide, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report set the median asking rent at **$1,250/mo** (2-bedroom median $1,100, 3-bedroom median $1,350), while RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report of larger professionally-managed apartment buildings put the citywide average at **$1,564/mo**. Both describe Cleveland as a whole rather than Fairfax specifically, since neither source breaks out rents by individual neighborhood.

headers: Typical monthly rent (our available homes, Cleveland-wide)
rows: Most common size we work with
Often with a basement and yard

body: Fairfax benefits from proximity to two major transit corridors: the **HealthLine** bus rapid transit route runs along Euclid Avenue just north of the neighborhood, connecting Public Square Downtown to University Circle, and RTA bus routes run along Cedar Avenue and Woodland Avenue through the neighborhood itself. The Cleveland Clinic main campus sits at the neighborhood's northern edge, and Downtown Cleveland is roughly 2-3 miles west via Cedar Avenue or Community College Boulevard.

items: **Transit:** RTA bus routes on Cedar and Woodland Avenues; HealthLine BRT nearby on Euclid Avenue
**Landmark:** Adjacent to the Cleveland Clinic main campus

body: Every rental home we work with in Fairfax and the surrounding 44104 area accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready — this is one of our core service areas. Keep in mind that the City of Cleveland does not have a source-of-income protection ordinance, so voucher acceptance here is a landlord choice rather than a legal requirement; Cleveland's 2024 "Pay to Stay" ordinance notably left source-of-income protections out of its final language. Applicants can start a Housing Choice Voucher application year-round through [CMHA's online portal](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php). For a full walkthrough of income limits, inspections, and the application process, see our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/).

Fairfax sits between the [Central](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-central-cleveland/) neighborhood and Hough, both areas where we also work with rental homes. If you're comparing options across the east side, our [Houses for Rent in Cleveland OH](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page rounds up rent ranges citywide, and the [Cleveland rentals hub](/cleveland-rentals/) links out to every neighborhood we cover.

If you need a specific bedroom count or a firmer budget ceiling, tell us when you [book a showing](/p/book-showing) — we can point you toward comparable homes elsewhere in our East Cleveland selection if nothing in Fairfax currently matches.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Fairfax?
faq.a: Yes. Fairfax (44104) is one of our core East Cleveland service areas, and every home we work with there accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Because Cleveland has no source-of-income law, voucher acceptance varies by landlord — book a showing to see what's currently available.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Fairfax?
faq.a: Across our Cleveland selection, 2-bedroom homes typically run $750-$1,100 and 3-bedroom homes $1,100-$1,500, with an overall range of $700-$1,800. Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report put the median asking rent at $1,250/mo, though neighborhood-level figures aren't separately published.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Fairfax in?
faq.a: Fairfax is in ZIP code 44104 on Cleveland's east side, directly adjacent to the Cleveland Clinic's main campus.

faq.q: How close is Fairfax to the Cleveland Clinic?
faq.a: Fairfax is immediately adjacent to the Cleveland Clinic's main campus, which sits at the neighborhood's northern edge. The Clinic is the region's largest employer, and its campus is directly reachable by RTA bus and the nearby HealthLine on Euclid Avenue.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with a lot of homes in Fairfax?
faq.a: Yes, the 44104 ZIP code covering Fairfax and Central is one of our higher-volume Cleveland service areas. Specific availability changes regularly, so book a showing or start an application to see current options.
